verses.latin_vulgate: Mortuus est autem Samuel, et congregatus est universus Israël, et planxerunt eum, et sepelierunt eum in domo sua in Ramatha. Consurgensque David descendit in desertum Pharan.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣מׇת שְׁמוּאֵ֔ל וַיִּקָּבְצ֤וּ כׇל־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ וַיִּסְפְּדוּ־ל֔וֹ וַיִּקְבְּרֻ֥הוּ בְּבֵית֖וֹ בָּרָמָ֑ה וַיָּ֣קׇם דָּוִ֔ד וַיֵּ֖רֶד אֶל־מִדְבַּ֥ר פָּארָֽן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Samuel died, and all Israel was gathered together, and they mourned for him, and buried him in his house in Ramatha. And David rose, and went down into the wilderness of Pharan.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Samuel kuoli, ja koko Israel kokoontui murehtimaan häntä ja hautasi hänen omaan huoneeseensa Ramaan. Mutta David nousi ja meni alas Paranin korpeen.
verses.king_james: And Samuel died; and all the Israelites were gathered together, and lamented him, and buried him in his house at Ramah. And David arose, and went down to the wilderness of Paran.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem vir quispiam in solitudine Maon, et possessio ejus in Carmelo, et homo ille magnus nimis : erantque ei oves tria millia, et mille capræ : et accidit ut tonderetur grex ejus in Carmelo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִ֨ישׁ בְּמָע֜וֹן וּמַעֲשֵׂ֣הוּ בַכַּרְמֶ֗ל וְהָאִישׁ֙ גָּד֣וֹל מְאֹ֔ד וְל֛וֹ צֹ֥אן שְׁלֹשֶׁת־אֲלָפִ֖ים וְאֶ֣לֶף עִזִּ֑ים וַיְהִ֛י בִּגְזֹ֥ז אֶת־צֹאנ֖וֹ בַּכַּרְמֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now there was a certain man in the wilderness of Maon, and his possessions were in Carmel, and the man was very great: and he had three thousand sheep, and a thousand goats: and it happened that he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja yksi mies asui Maonissa, jolla myös oli tekemistä Karmelissa, ja se mies oli sangen rikas, ja hänellä oli kolmetuhatta lammasta ja tuhannen vuohta; ja hän keritsi lampaitansa Karmelissa.
verses.king_james: And there was a man in Maon, whose possessions were in Carmel; and the man was very great, and he had three thousand sheep, and a thousand goats: and he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nomen autem viri illius erat Nabal. Et nomen uxoris ejus Abigail : eratque mulier illa prudentissima, et speciosa : porro vir ejus durus, et pessimus, et malitiosus : erat autem de genere Caleb.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשֵׁ֤ם הָאִישׁ֙ נָבָ֔ל וְשֵׁ֥ם אִשְׁתּ֖וֹ אֲבִגָ֑יִל וְהָאִשָּׁ֤ה טֽוֹבַת־שֶׂ֙כֶל֙ וִ֣יפַת תֹּ֔אַר וְהָאִ֥ישׁ קָשֶׁ֛ה וְרַ֥ע מַעֲלָלִ֖ים וְה֥וּא (כלבו) [כָֽלִבִּֽי]׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now the name of the man was Nabal: and the name of his wife was Abigail. And she was a prudent and very comely woman: but her husband was churlish, and very bad and ill natured: and he was of the house of Caleb.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja miehen nimi oli Nabal, ja hänen emäntänsä nimi Abigail; ja se oli toimellinen vaimo ja kaunis kasvoilta, mutta mies oli sangen kova ja paha töissänsä, ja hän oli Kalebin sukua.
verses.king_james: Now the name of the man was Nabal; and the name of his wife Abigail: and she was a woman of good understanding, and of a beautiful countenance: but the man was churlish and evil in his doings; and he was of the house of Caleb.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo audisset David in deserto quod tonderet Nabal gregem suum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֥ע דָּוִ֖ד בַּמִּדְבָּ֑ר כִּי־גֹזֵ֥ז נָבָ֖ל אֶת־צֹאנֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when David heard in the wilderness, that Nabal was shearing his sheep,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin David kuuli korvessa Nabalin keritsevän lampaitansa,
verses.king_james: And David heard in the wilderness that Nabal did shear his sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: misit decem juvenes, et dixit eis : Ascendite in Carmelum, et venietis ad Nabal, et salutabitis eum ex nomine meo pacifice.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֥ח דָּוִ֖ד עֲשָׂרָ֣ה נְעָרִ֑ים וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֜ד לַנְּעָרִ֗ים עֲל֤וּ כַרְמֶ֙לָה֙ וּבָאתֶ֣ם אֶל־נָבָ֔ל וּשְׁאֶלְתֶּם־ל֥וֹ בִשְׁמִ֖י לְשָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: He sent ten young men, and said to them: Go up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and salute him in my name with peace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Lähetti hän kymmenen nuorukaista ja sanoi heille: menkäät Karmeliin, ja kuin te tulette Nabalin tykö, niin tervehtikäät häntä minun puolestani ystävällisesti,
verses.king_james: And David sent out ten young men, and David said unto the young men, Get you up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicetis : Sit fratribus meis et tibi pax, et domui tuæ pax, et omnibus, quæcumque habes, sit pax.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲמַרְתֶּ֥ם כֹּ֖ה לֶחָ֑י וְאַתָּ֤ה שָׁלוֹם֙ וּבֵיתְךָ֣ שָׁל֔וֹם וְכֹ֥ל אֲשֶׁר־לְךָ֖ שָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And you shall say: Peace be to my brethren, and to thee, and peace to thy house, and peace to all that thou hast.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanokaat: terve! rauha olkoon sinulle, ja rauha huoneelles, ja kaikille mitä sinulle on, olkoon rauha!
verses.king_james: And thus shall ye say to him that liveth in prosperity, Peace be both to thee, and peace be to thine house, and peace be unto all that thou hast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivi quod tonderent pastores tui, qui erant nobiscum in deserto : numquam eis molesti fuimus, nec aliquando defuit quidquam eis de grege, omni tempore quo fuerunt nobiscum in Carmelo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֣ה שָׁמַ֔עְתִּי כִּ֥י גֹזְזִ֖ים לָ֑ךְ עַתָּ֗ה הָרֹעִ֤ים אֲשֶׁר־לְךָ֙ הָי֣וּ עִמָּ֔נוּ לֹ֣א הֶכְלַמְנ֗וּם וְלֹֽא־נִפְקַ֤ד לָהֶם֙ מְא֔וּמָה כׇּל־יְמֵ֖י הֱיוֹתָ֥ם בַּכַּרְמֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have heard that thy shepherds that were with us in the desert were shearing: we never molested them, neither was there ought missing to them of the flock at any time, all the while they were with us in Carmel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen nyt saanut kuulla, että sinulla on lammasten keritsiät: katso, paimenet jotka sinulla on, olivat meidän tykönämme, ja emme heitä häväisseet, ja ei heiltä mitään puuttunut niinkauvan kuin he olivat Karmelissa.
verses.king_james: And now I have heard that thou hast shearers: now thy shepherds which were with us, we hurt them not, neither was there ought missing unto them, all the while they were in Carmel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interroga pueros tuos, et indicabunt tibi. Nunc ergo inveniant pueri tui gratiam in oculis tuis : in die enim bona venimus : quodcumque invenerit manus tua, da servis tuis, et filio tuo David.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁאַ֨ל אֶת־נְעָרֶ֜יךָ וְיַגִּ֣ידוּ לָ֗ךְ וְיִמְצְא֨וּ הַנְּעָרִ֥ים חֵן֙ בְּעֵינֶ֔יךָ כִּֽי־עַל־י֥וֹם ט֖וֹב בָּ֑נוּ תְּנָה־נָּ֗א אֵת֩ אֲשֶׁ֨ר תִּמְצָ֤א יָֽדְךָ֙ לַעֲבָדֶ֔יךָ וּלְבִנְךָ֖ לְדָוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Ask thy servants, and they will tell thee. Now therefore let thy servants find favour in thy eyes: for we are come in a good day, whatsoever thy hand shall find give to thy servants, and to thy son David.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kysy palvelioiltas, he sanovat sinulle. Niin anna siis nuorukaisten löytää armo sinun kasvois edessä, sillä me olemme tulleet hyvään aikaan: anna siis palvelioilles ja pojalles Davidille, mitä sinun kätes löytää!
verses.king_james: Ask thy young men, and they will shew thee. Wherefore let the young men find favour in thine eyes: for we come in a good day: give, I pray thee, whatsoever cometh to thine hand unto thy servants, and to thy son David.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque venissent pueri David, locuti sunt ad Nabal omnia verba hæc ex nomine David : et siluerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ נַעֲרֵ֣י דָוִ֔ד וַיְדַבְּר֧וּ אֶל־נָבָ֛ל כְּכׇל־הַדְּבָרִ֥ים הָאֵ֖לֶּה בְּשֵׁ֣ם דָּוִ֑ד וַיָּנֽוּחוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when David's servants came, they spoke to Nabal all these words in David's name, and then held their peace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin Davidin palveliat sinne tulivat ja puhuivat kaikki nämät sanat Nabalille Davidin puolesta, niin he vaikenivat.
verses.king_james: And when David’s young men came, they spake to Nabal according to all those words in the name of David, and ceased.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Nabal pueris David, ait : Quis est David ? et quis est filius Isai ? hodie increverunt servi qui fugiunt dominos suos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֨עַן נָבָ֜ל אֶת־עַבְדֵ֤י דָוִד֙ וַיֹּ֔אמֶר מִ֥י דָוִ֖ד וּמִ֣י בֶן־יִשָׁ֑י הַיּוֹם֙ רַבּ֣וּ עֲבָדִ֔ים הַמִּתְפָּ֣רְצִ֔ים אִ֖ישׁ מִפְּנֵ֥י אֲדֹנָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Nabal answering the servants of David, said: Who is David? and what is the son of Isai? servants are multiplied now days who flee from their masters.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Nabal vastasi Davidin palvelioita ja sanoi: kuka on David? ja kuka on Isain poika? Nyt on monta palveliaa, jotka jättävät isäntänsä.
verses.king_james: And Nabal answered David’s servants, and said, Who is David? and who is the son of Jesse? there be many servants now a days that break away every man from his master.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tollam ergo panes meos, et aquas meas, et carnes pecorum quæ occidi tonsoribus meis, et dabo viris quos nescio unde sint ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלָקַחְתִּ֤י אֶת־לַחְמִי֙ וְאֶת־מֵימַ֔י וְאֵת֙ טִבְחָתִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר טָבַ֖חְתִּי לְגֹֽזְזָ֑י וְנָֽתַתִּי֙ לַאֲנָשִׁ֔ים אֲשֶׁר֙ לֹ֣א יָדַ֔עְתִּי אֵ֥י מִזֶּ֖ה הֵֽמָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Shall I then take my bread, and my water, and the flesh of my cattle, which I have killed for my shearers, and give to men whom I know not whence they are?
verses.finnish_biblia: Pitääkö minun ottaman leipäni, veteni ja teuraani, jonka minä keritsiöilleni olen teurastanut, ja antaman miehille, joita en minä tiedä, kusta he tulleet ovat?
verses.king_james: Shall I then take my bread, and my water, and my flesh that I have killed for my shearers, and give it unto men, whom I know not whence they be?

verses.latin_vulgate: Regressi sunt itaque pueri David per viam suam, et reversi venerunt, et nuntiaverunt ei omnia verba quæ dixerat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּהַפְכ֥וּ נַעֲרֵֽי־דָוִ֖ד לְדַרְכָּ֑ם וַיָּשֻׁ֙בוּ֙ וַיָּבֹ֔אוּ וַיַּגִּ֣דוּ ל֔וֹ כְּכֹ֖ל הַדְּבָרִ֥ים הָאֵֽלֶּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: So the servants of David went back their way, and returning came and told him all the words that he said.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Davidin palveliat palasivat tiellensä, ja tultuansa taas Davidin tykö sanoivat he hänelle kaikki nämät sanat.
verses.king_james: So David’s young men turned their way, and went again, and came and told him all those sayings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc ait David pueris suis : Accingatur unusquisque gladio suo. Et accincti sunt singuli gladiis suis, accinctusque est et David ense suo : et secuti sunt David quasi quadringenti viri : porro ducenti remanserunt ad sarcinas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ דָּוִ֨ד לַאֲנָשָׁ֜יו חִגְר֣וּ ׀ אִ֣ישׁ אֶת־חַרְבּ֗וֹ וַֽיַּחְגְּרוּ֙ אִ֣ישׁ אֶת־חַרְבּ֔וֹ וַיַּחְגֹּ֥ר גַּם־דָּוִ֖ד אֶת־חַרְבּ֑וֹ וַֽיַּעֲל֣וּ ׀ אַחֲרֵ֣י דָוִ֗ד כְּאַרְבַּ֤ע מֵאוֹת֙ אִ֔ישׁ וּמָאתַ֖יִם יָשְׁב֥וּ עַל־הַכֵּלִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then David said to his young men: Let every man gird on his sword. And they girded on every man his sword. And David also girded on his sword: and there followed David about four hundred men, and two hundred remained with the baggage.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi David miehillensä: vyöttäkään kukin miekkansa vyöllensä. Niin vyötti jokainen miekkansa vyöllensä, ja David vyötti myös miekkansa vyöllensä; ja läksi Davidin kanssa liki neljäsataa miestä, mutta kaksisataa jäi kaluin tykö.
verses.king_james: And David said unto his men, Gird ye on every man his sword. And they girded on every man his sword; and David also girded on his sword: and there went up after David about four hundred men; and two hundred abode by the stuff.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abigail autem uxori Nabal nuntiavit unus de pueris suis, dicens : Ecce David misit nuntios de deserto, ut benedicerent domino nostro : et aversatus est eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלַאֲבִיגַ֙יִל֙ אֵ֣שֶׁת נָבָ֔ל הִגִּ֧יד נַֽעַר־אֶחָ֛ד מֵהַנְּעָרִ֖ים לֵאמֹ֑ר הִנֵּ֣ה שָׁלַח֩ דָּוִ֨ד מַלְאָכִ֧ים ׀ מֵהַמִּדְבָּ֛ר לְבָרֵ֥ךְ אֶת־אֲדֹנֵ֖ינוּ וַיָּ֥עַט בָּהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But one of the servants told, Abigail, the wife of Nabal, saying: Behold, David sent messengers out of the wilderness, to salute our master: and he rejected them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta yksi palvelioista ilmoitti Abigailille, Nabalin emännälle, sanoen: katso, David on lähettänyt sanansaattajat korvesta siunaamaan isäntäämme, mutta hän tiuskui heitä.
verses.king_james: But one of the young men told Abigail, Nabal’s wife, saying, Behold, David sent messengers out of the wilderness to salute our master; and he railed on them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Homines isti boni satis fuerant nobis, et non molesti : nec quidquam aliquando periit omni tempore quo fuimus conversati cum eis in deserto :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָ֣אֲנָשִׁ֔ים טֹבִ֥ים לָ֖נוּ מְאֹ֑ד וְלֹ֤א הׇכְלַ֙מְנוּ֙ וְלֹֽא־פָקַ֣דְנֽוּ מְא֔וּמָה כׇּל־יְמֵי֙ הִתְהַלַּ֣כְנוּ אִתָּ֔ם בִּֽהְיוֹתֵ֖נוּ בַּשָּׂדֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: These men were very good to us, and gave us no trouble: Neither did we ever lose any thing all the time that we conversed with them in the desert.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ne miehet olivat meille aivan hyvät ja ei meitä pahoin puhutelleet, ja ei meiltä mitään puuttunut niinkauvan kuin me vaelsimme heidän tykönänsä kedolla ollessamme.
verses.king_james: But the men were very good unto us, and we were not hurt, neither missed we any thing, as long as we were conversant with them, when we were in the fields:

verses.latin_vulgate: pro muro erant nobis tam in nocte quam in die, omnibus diebus quibus pavimus apud eos greges.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חוֹמָה֙ הָי֣וּ עָלֵ֔ינוּ גַּם־לַ֖יְלָה גַּם־יוֹמָ֑ם כׇּל־יְמֵ֛י הֱיוֹתֵ֥נוּ עִמָּ֖ם רֹעִ֥ים הַצֹּֽאן׃
verses.douay_rheims: They were a wall unto us, both by night and day, all the while we were with them keeping the sheep.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta he ovat olleet meidän turvamme yötä ja päivää, niinkauvan kuin me kaitsimme lampaitamme heidän tykönänsä.
verses.king_james: They were a wall unto us both by night and day, all the while we were with them keeping the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam ob rem considera, et recogita quid facias : quoniam completa est malitia adversum virum tuum, et adversum domum tuam, et ipse est filius Belial, ita ut nemo possit ei loqui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֗ה דְּעִ֤י וּרְאִי֙ מַֽה־תַּעֲשִׂ֔י כִּֽי־כָלְתָ֧ה הָרָעָ֛ה אֶל־אֲדֹנֵ֖ינוּ וְעַ֣ל כׇּל־בֵּית֑וֹ וְהוּא֙ בֶּן־בְּלִיַּ֔עַל מִדַּבֵּ֖ר אֵלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore consider, and think what thou hast to do: for evil is determined against thy husband, and against thy house, and he is a son of Belial, so that no man can speak to him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin ajattele nyt ja katso mitäs teet; sillä pahuutta on totisesti tarjona meidän isännällemme ja kaikelle hänen huoneellensa, vaan hän on tyly mies, jota ei yksikään tohdi puhutella.
verses.king_james: Now therefore know and consider what thou wilt do; for evil is determined against our master, and against all his household: for he is such a son of Belial, that a man cannot speak to him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Festinavit igitur Abigail, et tulit ducentos panes, et duos utres vini, et quinque arietes coctos, et quinque sata polentæ, et centum ligaturas uvæ passæ, et ducentas massas caricarum, et posuit super asinos :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתְּמַהֵ֣ר (אבוגיל) [אֲבִיגַ֡יִל] וַתִּקַּח֩ מָאתַ֨יִם לֶ֜חֶם וּשְׁנַ֣יִם נִבְלֵי־יַ֗יִן וְחָמֵ֨שׁ צֹ֤אן (עשוות) [עֲשׂוּיוֹת֙] וְחָמֵ֤שׁ סְאִים֙ קָלִ֔י וּמֵאָ֥ה צִמֻּקִ֖ים וּמָאתַ֣יִם דְּבֵלִ֑ים וַתָּ֖שֶׂם עַל־הַחֲמֹרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Abigail made haste and took two hundred loaves, and two vessels of wine, and five sheep ready dressed, and five measures of parched corn, and a hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of dry figs, and laid them upon asses:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Abigail kiiruhti ja otti kaksisataa leipää, ja kaksi leiliä viinaa, ja viisi keitettyä lammasta, ja viisi vakkaista tuletettuja jauhoja, ja sata rusinarypälettä, ja kaksisataa rypälettä fikunia, ja pani aasein päälle,
verses.king_james: Then Abigail made haste, and took two hundred loaves, and two bottles of wine, and five sheep ready dressed, and five measures of parched corn, and an hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs, and laid them on asses.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixitque pueris suis : Præcedite me : ecce ego post tergum sequar vos : viro autem suo Nabal non indicavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֤אמֶר לִנְעָרֶ֙יהָ֙ עִבְר֣וּ לְפָנַ֔י הִנְנִ֖י אַחֲרֵיכֶ֣ם בָּאָ֑ה וּלְאִישָׁ֥הּ נָבָ֖ל לֹ֥א הִגִּֽידָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she said to her servants: Go before me: behold, I will follow after you: but she told not her husband, Nabal.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoi palvelioillensa: menkäät minun edelläni, katso, minä tulen perässänne. Ja ei hän miehellensä Nabalille sitä ilmoittanut.
verses.king_james: And she said unto her servants, Go on before me; behold, I come after you. But she told not her husband Nabal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo ascendisset asinum, et descenderet ad radices montis, David et viri ejus descendebant in occursum ejus : quibus et illa occurrit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֞ה הִ֣יא ׀ רֹכֶ֣בֶת עַֽל־הַחֲמ֗וֹר וְיֹרֶ֙דֶת֙ בְּסֵ֣תֶר הָהָ֔ר וְהִנֵּ֤ה דָוִד֙ וַאֲנָשָׁ֔יו יֹרְדִ֖ים לִקְרָאתָ֑הּ וַתִּפְגֹ֖שׁ אֹתָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when she had gotten upon an ass, and was coming down to the foot of the mountain, David and his men came down over against her, and she met them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän ajoi aasilla ja tuli vuoren varjoon, katso, David ja hänen väkensä tulivat vuorilta alas häntä vastaan; ja hän kohtasi heitä.
verses.king_james: And it was so, as she rode on the ass, that she came down by the covert of the hill, and, behold, David and his men came down against her; and she met them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait David : Vere frustra servavi omnia quæ hujus erant in deserto, et non periit quidquam de cunctis quæ ad eum pertinebant : et reddidit mihi malum pro bono.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדָוִ֣ד אָמַ֗ר אַךְ֩ לַשֶּׁ֨קֶר שָׁמַ֜רְתִּי אֶֽת־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֤ר לָזֶה֙ בַּמִּדְבָּ֔ר וְלֹא־נִפְקַ֥ד מִכׇּל־אֲשֶׁר־ל֖וֹ מְא֑וּמָה וַיָּשֶׁב־לִ֥י רָעָ֖ה תַּ֥חַת טוֹבָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said: Truly in vain have I kept all that belonged to this fellow in the wilderness, and nothing was lost of all that pertained unto him: and he hath returned me evil for good.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta David oli sanonut: minä olen turhaan tallella pitänyt kaikki, mitä hänellä oli korvessa, ja ei ole mitään puuttunut kaikista mitä hänellä oli; ja hän kostaa minun hyvät työni pahalla.
verses.king_james: Now David had said, Surely in vain have I kept all that this fellow hath in the wilderness, so that nothing was missed of all that pertained unto him: and he hath requited me evil for good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc faciat Deus inimicis David, et hæc addat, si reliquero de omnibus quæ ad ipsum pertinent usque mane mingentem ad parietem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּה־יַעֲשֶׂ֧ה אֱלֹהִ֛ים לְאֹיְבֵ֥י דָוִ֖ד וְכֹ֣ה יֹסִ֑יף אִם־אַשְׁאִ֧יר מִכׇּל־אֲשֶׁר־ל֛וֹ עַד־הַבֹּ֖קֶר מַשְׁתִּ֥ין בְּקִֽיר׃
verses.douay_rheims: May God do so and so, and add more to the foes of David, if I leave of all that belong to him till the morning, any that pisseth against the wall.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumala tehköön näitä Davidin vihamiehille ja vielä suurempia, jos minä jätän hänelle huomeneksi kaikista, mitä hänellä on, jonkun, joka vetensä seinään heittää.
verses.king_james: So and more also do God unto the enemies of David, if I leave of all that pertain to him by the morning light any that pisseth against the wall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem vidisset Abigail David, festinavit, et descendit de asino, et procidit coram David super faciem suam, et adoravit super terram,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֤רֶא אֲבִיגַ֙יִל֙ אֶת־דָּוִ֔ד וַתְּמַהֵ֕ר וַתֵּ֖רֶד מֵעַ֣ל הַחֲמ֑וֹר וַתִּפֹּ֞ל לְאַפֵּ֤י דָוִד֙ עַל־פָּנֶ֔יהָ וַתִּשְׁתַּ֖חוּ אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when Abigail saw David, she made haste and lighted off the ass, and fell before David, on her face, and adored upon the ground.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuin Abigail näki Davidin, kiiruhti hän ja astui nopiasti alas aasin päältä, lankesi kasvoillensa Davidin eteen ja kumarsi itsensä maahan,
verses.king_james: And when Abigail saw David, she hasted, and lighted off the ass, and fell before David on her face, and bowed herself to the ground,

verses.latin_vulgate: et cecidit ad pedes ejus, et dixit : In me sit, domine mi, hæc iniquitas : loquatur, obsecro, ancilla tua in auribus tuis, et audi verba famulæ tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּפֹּל֙ עַל־רַגְלָ֔יו וַתֹּ֕אמֶר בִּֽי־אֲנִ֥י אֲדֹנִ֖י הֶעָוֺ֑ן וּֽתְדַבֶּר־נָ֤א אֲמָֽתְךָ֙ בְּאׇזְנֶ֔יךָ וּשְׁמַ֕ע אֵ֖ת דִּבְרֵ֥י אֲמָתֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she fell at his feet, and said: Upon me let this iniquity be, my lord: let thy handmaid speak, I beseech thee, in thy ears, and hear the words of thy servant.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja lankesi hänen jalkainsa juureen ja sanoi: Ah herrani, minun olkoon tämä paha teko! ja anna piikas puhua korvais kuullen, ja kuule piikas sanaa:
verses.king_james: And fell at his feet, and said, Upon me, my lord, upon me let this iniquity be: and let thine handmaid, I pray thee, speak in thine audience, and hear the words of thine handmaid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne ponat, oro, dominus meus rex cor suum super virum istum iniquum Nabal : quoniam secundum nomen suum stultus est, et stultitia est cum eo : ego autem ancilla tua non vidi pueros tuos, domine mi, quos misisti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־נָ֣א יָשִׂ֣ים אֲדֹנִ֣י ׀ אֶת־לִבּ֡וֹ אֶל־אִישׁ֩ הַבְּלִיַּ֨עַל הַזֶּ֜ה עַל־נָבָ֗ל כִּ֤י כִשְׁמוֹ֙ כֶּן־ה֔וּא נָבָ֣ל שְׁמ֔וֹ וּנְבָלָ֖ה עִמּ֑וֹ וַֽאֲנִי֙ אֲמָ֣תְךָ֔ לֹ֥א רָאִ֛יתִי אֶת־נַעֲרֵ֥י אֲדֹנִ֖י אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁלָֽחְתָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let not my lord the king, I pray thee, regard this naughty man, Nabal: for according to his name, he is a fool, and folly is with him: but I, thy handmaid, did not see thy servants, my lord, whom thou sentest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älkään minun herrani asettako sydäntänsä tätä tylyä miestä Nabalia vastaan; sillä niinkuin hänen nimensä kuuluu, niin on hän: Nabal on hänen nimensä, tyhmyys on hänen kanssansa; mutta minä sinun piikas en ole nähnyt herrani palvelioita, jotka sinä olit lähettänyt.
verses.king_james: Let not my lord, I pray thee, regard this man of Belial, even Nabal: for as his name is, so is he; Nabal is his name, and folly is with him: but I thine handmaid saw not the young men of my lord, whom thou didst send.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo, domine mi, vivit Dominus, et vivit anima tua, qui prohibuit te ne venires in sanguinem, et salvavit manum tuam tibi : et nunc fiant sicut Nabal inimici tui, et qui quærunt domino meo malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֣ה אֲדֹנִ֗י חַי־יְהֹוָ֤ה וְחֵֽי־נַפְשְׁךָ֙ אֲשֶׁ֨ר מְנָעֲךָ֤ יְהֹוָה֙ מִבּ֣וֹא בְדָמִ֔ים וְהוֹשֵׁ֥עַ יָדְךָ֖ לָ֑ךְ וְעַתָּ֗ה יִֽהְי֤וּ כְנָבָל֙ אֹֽיְבֶ֔יךָ וְהַֽמְבַקְשִׁ֥ים אֶל־אֲדֹנִ֖י רָעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now therefore, my lord, the Lord liveth, and thy soul liveth, who hath withholden thee from coming to blood, and hath saved thy hand to thee: and now let thy enemies be as Nabal, and all they that seek evil to my lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta nyt, herrani, niin totta kuin Herra elää ja niin totta kuin sinun sielus elää, niin Herra on sinun estänyt, ettet verta vuodattanut eikä sinun kätes vapahtanut sinua. Mutta nyt olkoon sinun vihamiehes niin kuin Nabal ja kaikki jotka herralleni pahaa suovat.
verses.king_james: Now therefore, my lord, as the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, seeing the Lord hath withholden thee from coming to shed blood, and from avenging thyself with thine own hand, now let thine enemies, and they that seek evil to my lord, be as Nabal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quapropter suscipe benedictionem hanc, quam attulit ancilla tua tibi domino meo, et da pueris qui sequuntur te dominum meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּה֙ הַבְּרָכָ֣ה הַזֹּ֔את אֲשֶׁר־הֵבִ֥יא שִׁפְחָתְךָ֖ לַֽאדֹנִ֑י וְנִתְּנָה֙ לַנְּעָרִ֔ים הַמִּֽתְהַלְּכִ֖ים בְּרַגְלֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore receive this blessing, which thy handmaid hath brought to thee, my lord: and give it to the young men that follow thee, my lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on se siunaus, jonka sinun piikas minun herralleni tuonut on, annettaa palvelioille, jotka herrani kanssa vaeltavat.
verses.king_james: And now this blessing which thine handmaid hath brought unto my lord, let it even be given unto the young men that follow my lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aufer iniquitatem famulæ tuæ : faciens enim faciet Dominus tibi domino meo domum fidelem, quia prælia Domini, domine mi, tu præliaris : malitia ergo non inveniatur in te omnibus diebus vitæ tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׂ֥א נָ֖א לְפֶ֣שַׁע אֲמָתֶ֑ךָ כִּ֣י עָשֹֽׂה־יַעֲשֶׂה֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה לַאדֹנִ֜י בַּ֣יִת נֶאֱמָ֗ן כִּֽי־מִלְחֲמ֤וֹת יְהֹוָה֙ אֲדֹנִ֣י נִלְחָ֔ם וְרָעָ֛ה לֹא־תִמָּצֵ֥א בְךָ֖ מִיָּמֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Forgive the iniquity of thy handmaid: for the Lord will surely make for my lord a faithful house, because thou, my lord, fightest the battles of the Lord: let not evil therefore be found in thee all the days of thy life.
verses.finnish_biblia: Anna piialles tämä rikos anteeksi. Sillä Herra tosin tekee minun herralleni vahvan huoneen; sillä koska minun herrani sotii Herran sotia, niin ei yhtäkään pahuutta ole löydetty sinun tyköäs kaikkena sinun elinaikanas.
verses.king_james: I pray thee, forgive the trespass of thine handmaid: for the Lord will certainly make my lord a sure house; because my lord fighteth the battles of the Lord, and evil hath not been found in thee all thy days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim surrexerit aliquando homo persequens te, et quærens animam tuam, erit anima domini mei custodita quasi in fasciculo viventium apud Dominum Deum tuum : porro inimicorum tuorum anima rotabitur, quasi in impetu et circulo fundæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֤קׇם אָדָם֙ לִרְדׇפְךָ֔ וּלְבַקֵּ֖שׁ אֶת־נַפְשֶׁ֑ךָ וְֽהָיְתָה֩ נֶ֨פֶשׁ אֲדֹנִ֜י צְרוּרָ֣ה ׀ בִּצְר֣וֹר הַחַיִּ֗ים אֵ֚ת יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֶ֔יךָ וְאֵ֨ת נֶ֤פֶשׁ אֹיְבֶ֙יךָ֙ יְקַלְּעֶ֔נָּה בְּת֖וֹךְ כַּ֥ף הַקָּֽלַע׃
verses.douay_rheims: For if a man at any time shall rise, and persecute thee, and seek thy life, the soul of my lord shall be kept, as in the bundle of the living, with the Lord thy God: but the souls of thy enemies shall be whirled, as with the violence and whirling of a sling.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos joku ihminen asettaa itsensä vainoomaan sinua ja väijyy sinun sielus jälkeen, niin olkaan herrani sielu sidottu eläväin kimppuun Herran sinun Jumalas tykönä; mutta sinun vihollistes sielu pitää lingottaman lingolla.
verses.king_james: Yet a man is risen to pursue thee, and to seek thy soul: but the soul of my lord shall be bound in the bundle of life with the Lord thy God; and the souls of thine enemies, them shall he sling out, as out of the middle of a sling.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo fecerit Dominus tibi domino meo omnia quæ locutus est bona de te, et constituerit te ducem super Israël,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֗ה כִּֽי־יַעֲשֶׂ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ לַֽאדֹנִ֔י כְּכֹ֛ל אֲשֶׁר־דִּבֶּ֥ר אֶת־הַטּוֹבָ֖ה עָלֶ֑יךָ וְצִוְּךָ֥ לְנָגִ֖יד עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the Lord shall have done to thee, my lord, all the good that he hath spoken concerning thee, and shall have made thee prince over Israel,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Herra minun herralleni kaikki nämät hyvät tekevä on, niinkuin hän sinulle sanonut on, ja käskee sinun olla Israelin ruhtinaan;
verses.king_james: And it shall come to pass, when the Lord shall have done to my lord according to all the good that he hath spoken concerning thee, and shall have appointed thee ruler over Israel;

verses.latin_vulgate: non erit tibi hoc in singultum, et in scrupulum cordis domino meo, quod effuderis sanguinem innoxium, aut ipse te ultus fueris : et cum benefecerit Dominus domino meo, recordaberis ancillæ tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֣א תִהְיֶ֣ה זֹ֣את ׀ לְךָ֡ לְפוּקָה֩ וּלְמִכְשׁ֨וֹל לֵ֜ב לַאדֹנִ֗י וְלִשְׁפׇּךְ־דָּם֙ חִנָּ֔ם וּלְהוֹשִׁ֥יעַ אֲדֹנִ֖י ל֑וֹ וְהֵיטִ֤ב יְהֹוָה֙ לַֽאדֹנִ֔י וְזָכַרְתָּ֖ אֶת־אֲמָתֶֽךָ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: This shall not be an occasion of grief to thee, and a scruple of heart to my lord, that thou hast shed innocent blood, or hast revenged thyself: and when the Lord shall have done well by my lord, thou shalt remember thy handmaid.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin ei pidä se oleman sinulle minun herralleni lankeemiseksi ja mielen pahennukseksi, ettet vuodattanut verta ilman syytä ja auttanut itse sinuas; ja Herra on hyvää tekevä minun herralleni, ja sinä muistat piikaas.
verses.king_james: That this shall be no grief unto thee, nor offence of heart unto my lord, either that thou hast shed blood causeless, or that my lord hath avenged himself: but when the Lord shall have dealt well with my lord, then remember thine handmaid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait David ad Abigail : Benedictus Dominus Deus Israël, qui misit hodie te in occursum meum, et benedictum eloquium tuum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר דָּוִ֖ד לַאֲבִיגַ֑ל בָּר֤וּךְ יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁ֧ר שְׁלָחֵ֛ךְ הַיּ֥וֹם הַזֶּ֖ה לִקְרָאתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to Abigail: Blessed be the Lord the God of Israel, who sent thee this day to meet me, and blessed be thy speech:
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin sanoi David Abigailille: kiitetty olkoon Herra Israelin Jumala, joka sinun tänäpänä on lähettänyt minua vastaan.
verses.king_james: And David said to Abigail, Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, which sent thee this day to meet me:

verses.latin_vulgate: et benedicta tu, quæ prohibuisti me hodie ne irem ad sanguinem, et ulciscerer me manu mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבָר֥וּךְ טַעְמֵ֖ךְ וּבְרוּכָ֣ה אָ֑תְּ אֲשֶׁ֨ר כְּלִתִ֜נִי הַיּ֤וֹם הַזֶּה֙ מִבּ֣וֹא בְדָמִ֔ים וְהֹשֵׁ֥עַ יָדִ֖י לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And blessed be thou, who hast kept me to day from coming to blood, and revenging me with my own hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja siunattu olkoon sinun toimellinen puhees, ja siunattu olet sinä, joka minun tänäpänä estit verta vuodattamasta ja kostamasta omalla kädelläni.
verses.king_james: And blessed be thy advice, and blessed be thou, which hast kept me this day from coming to shed blood, and from avenging myself with mine own hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alioquin vivit Dominus Deus Israël, qui prohibuit me ne malum facerem tibi : nisi cito venisses in occursum mihi, non remansisset Nabal usque ad lucem matutinam mingens ad parietem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאוּלָ֗ם חַי־יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר מְנָעַ֔נִי מֵהָרַ֖ע אֹתָ֑ךְ כִּ֣י ׀ לוּלֵ֣י מִהַ֗רְתְּ (ותבאתי) [וַתָּבֹאת֙] לִקְרָאתִ֔י כִּ֣י אִם־נוֹתַ֧ר לְנָבָ֛ל עַד־א֥וֹר הַבֹּ֖קֶר מַשְׁתִּ֥ין בְּקִֽיר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Otherwise, as the Lord liveth, the God of Israel, who hath withholden me from doing thee any evil, if thou hadst not quickly come to meet me, there had not been left to Nabal by the morning light, any that pisseth against the wall.
verses.finnish_biblia: Totisesti, niin totta kuin Herra Israelin Jumala elää, se joka minun on tänäpänä estänyt sinulle pahaa tekemästä, jollet nopiasti olisi tullut minua vastaan, niin ei olisi Nabalille jäänyt huomeneksi yhtäkään seinään vetensä heittävää.
verses.king_james: For in very deed, as the Lord God of Israel liveth, which hath kept me back from hurting thee, except thou hadst hasted and come to meet me, surely there had not been left unto Nabal by the morning light any that pisseth against the wall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Suscepit ergo David de manu ejus omnia quæ attulerat ei, dixitque ei : Vade pacifice in domum tuam : ecce audivi vocem tuam, et honoravi faciem tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֤ח דָּוִד֙ מִיָּדָ֔הּ אֵ֥ת אֲשֶׁר־הֵבִ֖יאָה ל֑וֹ וְלָ֣הּ אָמַ֗ר עֲלִ֤י לְשָׁלוֹם֙ לְבֵיתֵ֔ךְ רְאִי֙ שָׁמַ֣עְתִּי בְקוֹלֵ֔ךְ וָאֶשָּׂ֖א פָּנָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David received at her hand all that she had brought him, and said to her: Go in peace into thy house, behold I have heard thy voice, and honoured thy face.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin otti David hänen kädestänsä, mitä hän oli hänelle tuonut, ja sanoi hänelle: mene rauhassa sinun huoneeses, ja katso, minä kuulin sinun äänes ja otin sinun hyväksi.
verses.king_james: So David received of her hand that which she had brought him, and said unto her, Go up in peace to thine house; see, I have hearkened to thy voice, and have accepted thy person.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem Abigail ad Nabal : et ecce erat ei convivium in domo ejus quasi convivium regis, et cor Nabal jucundum : erat enim ebrius nimis : et non indicavit ei verbum pusillum aut grande usque mane.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּבֹ֣א אֲבִיגַ֣יִל ׀ אֶל־נָבָ֡ל וְהִנֵּה־לוֹ֩ מִשְׁתֶּ֨ה בְּבֵית֜וֹ כְּמִשְׁתֵּ֣ה הַמֶּ֗לֶךְ וְלֵ֤ב נָבָל֙ ט֣וֹב עָלָ֔יו וְה֥וּא שִׁכֹּ֖ר עַד־מְאֹ֑ד וְלֹא־הִגִּ֣ידָה לּ֗וֹ דָּבָ֥ר קָטֹ֛ן וְגָד֖וֹל עַד־א֥וֹר הַבֹּֽקֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Abigail came to Nabal: and behold he had a feast in his house, like the feast of a king: and Nabal's heart was merry, for he was very drunk: and she told him nothing less or more until morning.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin Abigail tuli Nabalin tykö, katso, niin hänellä oli huoneessansa pito, niinkuin kuninkaallinen pito, ja Nabalin sydän oli iloinen, sillä hän oli juuri kovin juovuksissa; mutta ei hän sanonut hänelle vähää eli paljoa huomeneen asti.
verses.king_james: And Abigail came to Nabal; and, behold, he held a feast in his house, like the feast of a king; and Nabal’s heart was merry within him, for he was very drunken: wherefore she told him nothing, less or more, until the morning light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Diluculo autem cum digessisset vinum Nabal, indicavit ei uxor sua verba hæc : et emortuum est cor ejus intrinsecus, et factus est quasi lapis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י בַבֹּ֗קֶר בְּצֵ֤את הַיַּ֙יִן֙ מִנָּבָ֔ל וַתַּגֶּד־ל֣וֹ אִשְׁתּ֔וֹ אֶת־הַדְּבָרִ֖ים הָאֵ֑לֶּה וַיָּ֤מׇת לִבּוֹ֙ בְּקִרְבּ֔וֹ וְה֖וּא הָיָ֥ה לְאָֽבֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: But early in the morning, when Nabal had digested his wine, his wife told him these words, and his heart died within him, and he became as a stone.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta huomeneltain, kuin Nabal oli viinasta kaljennut, sanoi hänen emäntänsä hänelle kaikki nämät; niin hänen sydämensä kuoli hänessä, että hän tuli niinkuin kivi.
verses.king_james: But it came to pass in the morning, when the wine was gone out of Nabal, and his wife had told him these things, that his heart died within him, and he became as a stone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque pertransissent decem dies, percussit Dominus Nabal, et mortuus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֖י כַּעֲשֶׂ֣רֶת הַיָּמִ֑ים וַיִּגֹּ֧ף יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶת־נָבָ֖ל וַיָּמֹֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And after ten days had passed, the Lord struck Nabal, and he died.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kymmenen päivän perästä löi Herra Nabalin, että hän kuoli.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass about ten days after, that the Lord smote Nabal, that he died.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod cum audisset David mortuum Nabal, ait : Benedictus Dominus, qui judicavit causam opprobrii mei de manu Nabal, et servum suum custodivit a malo, et malitiam Nabal reddidit Dominus in caput ejus. Misit ergo David, et locutus est ad Abigail, ut sumeret eam sibi in uxorem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֣ע דָּוִד֮ כִּ֣י מֵ֣ת נָבָל֒ וַיֹּ֡אמֶר בָּר֣וּךְ יְהֹוָ֡ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר רָב֩ אֶת־רִ֨יב חֶרְפָּתִ֜י מִיַּ֣ד נָבָ֗ל וְאֶת־עַבְדּוֹ֙ חָשַׂ֣ךְ מֵרָעָ֔ה וְאֵת֙ רָעַ֣ת נָבָ֔ל הֵשִׁ֥יב יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּרֹאשׁ֑וֹ וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח דָּוִד֙ וַיְדַבֵּ֣ר בַּאֲבִיגַ֔יִל לְקַחְתָּ֥הּ ל֖וֹ לְאִשָּֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when David had heard that Nabal was dead, he said: Blessed be the Lord, who hath judged the cause of my reproach, at the hand of Nabal, and hath kept his servant from evil, and the Lord hath returned the wickedness of Nabal upon his head. Then David sent and treated with Abigail, that he might take her to himself for a wife.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin David kuuli Nabalin kuolleeksi, sanoi hän: kiitetty olkoon Herra, joka on kostanut häväistykseni Nabalille ja on varjellut palveliansa pahasta, ja Herra on kääntänyt Nabalin pahuuden hänen päänsä päälle. Ja David lähetti ja antoi puhutella Abigailia, ottaaksensa häntä emännäksensä.
verses.king_james: And when David heard that Nabal was dead, he said, Blessed be the Lord, that hath pleaded the cause of my reproach from the hand of Nabal, and hath kept his servant from evil: for the Lord hath returned the wickedness of Nabal upon his own head. And David sent and communed with Abigail, to take her to him to wife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venerunt pueri David ad Abigail in Carmelum, et locuti sunt ad eam, dicentes : David misit nos ad te, ut accipiat te sibi in uxorem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֜אוּ עַבְדֵ֥י דָוִ֛ד אֶל־אֲבִיגַ֖יִל הַכַּרְמֶ֑לָה וַיְדַבְּר֤וּ אֵלֶ֙יהָ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר דָּוִד֙ שְׁלָחָ֣נוּ אֵלַ֔יִךְ לְקַחְתֵּ֥ךְ ל֖וֹ לְאִשָּֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David's servants came to Abigail, to Carmel, and spoke to her, saying: David hath sent us to thee, to take thee to himself for a wife.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Davidin palveliat tulivat Abigailin tykö Karmeliin, puhuttelivat he häntä ja sanoivat: David lähetti meitä sinun tykös, että hän ottais sinun emännäksensä.
verses.king_james: And when the servants of David were come to Abigail to Carmel, they spake unto her, saying, David sent us unto thee, to take thee to him to wife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ consurgens, adoravit prona in terram, et ait : Ecce famula tua sit in ancillam, ut lavet pedes servorum domini mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּ֕קׇם וַתִּשְׁתַּ֥חוּ אַפַּ֖יִם אָ֑רְצָה וַתֹּ֗אמֶר הִנֵּ֤ה אֲמָֽתְךָ֙ לְשִׁפְחָ֔ה לִרְחֹ֕ץ רַגְלֵ֖י עַבְדֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she arose, and bowed herself down with her face to the earth, and said: Behold, let thy servant be a handmaid, to wash the feet of the servants of my lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän nousi ja lankesi maahan kasvoillensa, sanoen: katso, tässä on sinun piikas palvelemaan ja pesemään herrani palveliain jalkoja.
verses.king_james: And she arose, and bowed herself on her face to the earth, and said, Behold, let thine handmaid be a servant to wash the feet of the servants of my lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et festinavit, et surrexit Abigail, et ascendit super asinum, et quinque puellæ ierunt cum ea, pedissequæ ejus, et secuta est nuntios David : et facta est illi uxor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתְּמַהֵ֞ר וַתָּ֣קׇם אֲבִיגַ֗יִל וַתִּרְכַּב֙ עַֽל־הַחֲמ֔וֹר וְחָמֵשׁ֙ נַעֲרֹתֶ֔יהָ הַהֹלְכ֖וֹת לְרַגְלָ֑הּ וַתֵּ֗לֶךְ אַֽחֲרֵי֙ מַלְאֲכֵ֣י דָוִ֔ד וַתְּהִי־ל֖וֹ לְאִשָּֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Abigail arose, and made haste, and got upon an ass, and five damsels went with her, her waiting maids, and she followed the messengers of David, and became his wife.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Abigail kiiruhti itsensä, ja valmisti hänensä, ja ajoi aasilla, ja otti viisi piikaansa kanssansa, jotka kävivät hänen jälissänsä; ja hän seurasi Davidin sanansaattajia ja tuli hänen emännäksensä.
verses.king_james: And Abigail hasted, and arose, and rode upon an ass, with five damsels of hers that went after her; and she went after the messengers of David, and became his wife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et Achinoam accepit David de Jezraël : et fuit utraque uxor ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־אֲחִינֹ֛עַם לָקַ֥ח דָּוִ֖ד מִֽיִּזְרְעֶ֑אל וַתִּהְיֶ֛יןָ גַּֽם־שְׁתֵּיהֶ֥ן ל֖וֹ לְנָשִֽׁים׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Moreover David took also Achinoam of Jezrahel: and they were both of them his wives.
verses.finnish_biblia: David otti myös Ahinoamin Jisreelistä; ja olivat ne molemmat hänen emäntänsä.
verses.king_james: David also took Ahinoam of Jezreel; and they were also both of them his wives.

verses.latin_vulgate: Saul autem dedit Michol filiam suam, uxorem David, Phalti filio Lais, qui erat de Gallim.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשָׁא֗וּל נָתַ֛ן אֶת־מִיכַ֥ל בִּתּ֖וֹ אֵ֣שֶׁת דָּוִ֑ד לְפַלְטִ֥י בֶן־לַ֖יִשׁ אֲשֶׁ֥ר מִגַּלִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Saul gave Michol, his daughter, David's wife, to Phalti, the son of Lais, who was of Gallim.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Saul antoi tyttärensä Mikalin, Davidin emännän, Phaltille Laiksen pojalle Gallimista.
verses.king_james: But Saul had given Michal his daughter, David’s wife, to Phalti the son of Laish, which was of Gallim.
